__fixture__.name: compatibility mismatch refuses strong claims
__fixture__.scenario: A capability that is otherwise stable must refuse certified/supported language when the current environment tuple mismatches the supported envelope. The badge group remains text-first and export-safe.
contract_sections: Allowed combinations and denial / downgrade rules

support_class.explanation_sentence: The current environment tuple falls outside the certified envelope; the surface refuses a certified/supported claim in this mode and routes to compatible options.

lifecycle.explanation_sentence: Lifecycle maturity is separate from compatibility for the active tuple; the mismatch badge narrows what the surface may claim.

channel.explanation_sentence: Channel identity is shown as context; it does not override the mismatch refusal.

freshness.explanation_sentence: The mismatch is based on current evidence; the refusal is not a stale-cached artifact.

client_scope.explanation_sentence: This marketplace card is a desktop surface; other clients must carry their own scoped compatibility disclosure.

certified_archetype_state.explanation_sentence: The backing compatibility/certified-archetype row reports unsupported for this tuple; the surface refuses strong support claims accordingly.

compatibility_state.explanation_sentence: The current tuple does not match the supported envelope; users should be routed to compatible configurations or documented limitations.

expected_projection.export_text: Mismatch · Not certified · Live · 3 more (Lifecycle, Channel, Client scope)
